Video.summary: The ghouls get the '70 AAR Cuda 340 Six Barrel car completely disassembled and off to the media blaster. Mark disassembles and restores the right-hand door to the 71' 440 Phantom Cuda. In addition, the headliner and vinyl top is installed in the 71 'Phantasm' Tribute car.
Video.title: Every Rose Has Its Thorn

Video.summary: The ghouls get the '70 AAR Cuda 340 Six Barrel car completely disassembled and off to the media blaster. Mark disassembles and restores the right-hand door to the 71' 440 Phantom Cuda. In addition, the headliner and vinyl top is installed in the 71 'Phantasm' Tribute car.
Video.title: Every Rose Has Its Thorn

Video.summary: The owner of the '70 AAR Cuda 340 Six Barrel car visits the shop and learns about assembly-line markings. Mark checks the paint on the body of the '70 Cuda 383 Automatic FE5 Rallye Red Car and discovers a major problem.
Video.title: You Win Some …

Video.summary: The ghouls receives a '70 Barracuda Convertible to get a full restoration. Despite improbability, Mark straightens the fender for the '71 440 Phantom Cuda and the crew continues the restoration of the '71 'Phantasm' Tribute car.
Video.title: Cuda Madness

Video.summary: The ghouls receives a '70 Barracuda Convertible to get a full restoration. Despite improbability, Mark straightens the fender for the '71 440 Phantom Cuda and the crew continues the restoration of the '71 'Phantasm' Tribute car.
Video.title: Cuda Madness

Video.summary: The ghouls detail and assemble the suspension of the '70 AAR Cuda 340 Six Barrel car. The owners of the '70 Plymouth Superbird, in the queue for resurrection, visit the shop and Daren convinces the crew to throw a birthday party for Mark.
Video.title: Flippin' the Bird

Video.summary: Mark takes some time for himself and leaves Daren in charge of the shop. The crew gets the newly painted panels installed on the '70 Cuda 383 Automatic FE5 Rallye Red Car. And Mark discovers a chip on the '71 'Phantasm' Tribute car and no one will own up to it.
Video.title: Fish n' Chips

Video.summary: The fuel and brake lines are installed in the '71 'Phantasm' Tribute car. Daren quits and won't return until Mark begins the restoration of his '70 Plum Crazy Challenger. A phone-call from the Phantasm car owner forces Mark to make a promise he might not be able to keep.
Video.title: Game Day

Video.summary: The GYC crew is under the gun to get the '71 Cuda 340 Triple Black 'Phantasm' Tribute car finished by the end of the week. With the exhaust, ornamation, and the engine start still ahead of them, will they be able to finish the car before the director of the 'Phantasm' movie franchise arrives?
Video.title: Phantasm ‘Cuda

Video.summary: Nick Rutter shows you how to properly care for your wheels and tires
Video.title: Wheel & Tire Care

Video.summary: Nick Rutter shows you how to fix common issues with your automitve finish
Video.title: Common Problems

Video.summary: Matt and Bruno are working on a 2006 Dodge Ram with a ton of problems…but why just make the repairs? Go better than stock!
Video.title: Better Than Stock

Video.summary: Matt visits the Wheels for Hope Garage in Raleigh, NC and sees first hand how this organization is changing lives.
Video.title: Wheels For Hope

Video.summary: Matt and Bruno upgrade the Dana 35 rear end in a 2000 Jeep TJ to a much stronger Ford 8.8 one and do it for under $500.
Video.title: Budget Rear End

Video.summary: Every light on your dashboard means something. Matt and Bruno diagnose and repair a vehicle that came to the shop to them with warning lights.
Video.title: Warning Lights

Video.summary: Nobody likes to fix a truck on the side of the road. That’s why there’s roadside assistance. Matt and Bruno take a stock F250 and turn it into a purpose-built Road Ranger.
Video.title: Road Ranger

Video.summary: With two days and a ton of truck parts anything can happen. Matt and Bruno put together build teams to see who can complete the toughest rig.
Video.title: Jeep Build-Off Day 1

Video.summary: The Jeep Build-Off continues as the guys get a chance to finish their Jeeps and take them on a Geocaching race for all of the marbles.
Video.title: Jeep Build-Off Day 2

Video.summary: The Jeep Build-Off continues as the guys get a chance to finish their Jeeps and take them on a Geocaching race for all of the marbles.
Video.title: Jeep Build-Off Day 3

Video.summary: Grammy-winning musician Marty Robbins lived a double life, flawlessly crossing between the glitz of the Grand Ole Opry and track at Talladega. One of few musicians to ever achieve success in both genres, Ray is determined to return Marty’s iconic 777 car to the race track travel back to the 60’s and 70’s.
Video.title: MARTY ROBBINS

Video.summary: Legendary drag racer Don “The Snake” Prudhomme proves he is no stranger to the thrill of racing rivalry. Prudhomme’s career and subsequent competition with driver Tom “The Mongoose” McEwan earned the pair an unforgettable imprint on American pop culture. Legions of kids in the 60’s and 70’s flocked to the shelves for Snake and Mongoose Matchbox cars which helped make drag racing an undisputable American pastime. Famed director Ron Howard makes an appearance to discuss the incredible stories hidden within the world of Formula 1 Racing, the topic of his latest, epic feature ‘Rush”.
Video.title: HOT RODS

Video.summary: Ray finds Buried Treasures in the form of two race cars that had been left almost completely untouched since the last time they were on a race track. One is found sitting in a barn with the other trapped in an old car hauler. Upon uncovering the cars, we find both have big stories to tell.
Video.title: BURIED TREASURE

Video.summary: A journey to Indianapolis unveils the winning car of the 1955 Indy 500 sits on the roof of an auto mechanic shop. More than 50 years after it’s magic at the track, prominent Indy mechanic/owner Chris Paulsen begins to restore the car and Ray talks about the evolution of America’s illustrious Indy 500.
Video.title: INDY RESCUE

Video.summary: In the 1960s, Smokey Yunick, the most innovative mechanic in NASCAR history, sold his famous truck that hauled equipment & cars across the country. Ray is on a mission to uncover this storied truck that helped launch the beginning of NASCAR’s cross-country popularity.
Video.title: SMOKEY

Video.summary: Ray travels to Long Island, New York with the hopes of learning more about America’s first major auto racing competition. The race, which began at the turn of the 20th century, helped develop the first major American racing stars and drove American manufacturing to competitive standard with the highly progressive Europeans.
Video.title: VANDERBILT

Video.summary: Fred Dmuchowski is a New Jersey racing icon. He was a regular workingman who raced on weekends for fun and became a legend in his iconic 303 racer. Ray works to rebuild the 303 in homage to Fred who inspired him to pursue his career in motorsports.
Video.title: RAYS ROOTS

Video.summary: For Rick Hendrick, the Corvette inspired his first car purchase, which ultimately led to the creation of his car dealership empire and motorsports teams. Mr. H, as he goes by, gives us a rare look inside of his personal Corvette collection and takes us to General Motors World Headquarters to talk with GM Vice President of Global Design, Ed Welburn, about how this iconic car came about. Country superstar Brad Paisley makes a cameo appearance to talk about his prized ‘Vette and the influence of cars on his music.
Video.title: THE CORVETTE

Video.summary: The last car Elvis drove the evening he passed was his beloved Stutz Blackhawk. Ray discovers that the car has been preserved and heads to Graceland to check out this iconic piece of American music history. Ray and the team reveal the impeccable preservation of the car at Charlotte Motor Speedway with Rick Hendrick (“Mr. H”) and, arguably Elvis Presley’s biggest fan, Dale Earnhardt Jr. While at Graceland, Ray and the team also get an exclusive look at the paneled station wagon Elvis used to get in and out of Graceland in disguise. It’s an intimate look at the King’s incredible automobiles and their secrets that only Elvis knew.
Video.title: ELVIS VAULT

Video.summary: This episode takes an inside look at the famous racing L88 Corvette. From its history, to its successes and heartbreaks, to the stories of the men that drove it. Now, it’s a car world’s version of the Flinstones meet the Jetsons, as the Owens Corning Corvette, which clinched 22 straight race victories, challenges the new Corvette C7-R at Sebring International Raceway. The original Owens Corning team reunites for the challenge and our crew goes behind the scenes for the Corvette test drive of a lifetime.
Video.title: BAND OF BROTHERS

Video.summary: From break up to make up, Jeff Gordon and Ray reunite to go on a quest to find their Chevrolet Monte Carlo that claimed victory in their final race together at Phoenix, AZ in 1999. John Bickford, Jeff Gordon’s stepdad, makes the call that reunites the old team. Jeff and Ray look to bum parts off their boss, Rick Hendrick, to make their vision a reality: get the car, replace the parts as they were, make it run and get it to auction.
Video.title: BREAK UP TO MAKEUP

Video.summary: One of the greatest battles in NASCAR history was Hall of Famer Richie Evans and rival speedster Jerry Cook. Ray is determined to uncover and revive the cars that made these men famous.Ray purchases one of Richie’s original #61 modifieds from Jimmy Spencer and restores it to its original glory while simultaneously building a replica of Jerry Cook’s #38. Throughout the build process, Ray uncovers stories about these legends through the eyes of guys who were in Rome, NY to witness it firsthand, like Robin Pemberton and Steve Hmiel.Both cars will hit the track at Martinsville, site of one of the greatest races in modified history and the tragic death of Richie Evans himself. Legends live again, as Ray recreates one of the racing world’s most celebrated rivalries on a track that has seen it all.
Video.title: BATTLE FOR ROME

Video.summary: General Motors VP of global design Ed Welburn takes AmeriCarna behind the scenes to talk about the history and development of the company’s concept cars that never made it to production. In a rare tour of the factory’s inner design studio, Ray gets an exclusive look behind the creative curtain at the GM plant in Detroit.
Video.title: SECRETS OF GM

Video.summary: In this episode, Ray celebrates the 50th birthday of the Ford Mustang through the lens of three distinct Mustang owners, who each hold a slice of the car’s incredible history. In 1964, Chicago schoolteacher Gail Wise unknowingly purchased the first ever Mustang 3 days before the car became available to the general public. Her husband reveals the incredible restoration that brought the earliest Mustang ever sold back to its 60’s glory. (Ford Motor Company knows she has the car and has verified it was sold before it was legal to sell.) John Grafelman is a farmer in Illinois and life-long Ford lover. A few years ago he found an old car hidden in a barn. It took some time, but he eventually convinced his friend to sell it to him. After some detective work, he discovers he purchased 1968 Ford Design Chief Larry Shinoda’s personal car that he tinkered with to invent the BOSS 302.Lastly, Ray travels to Dayton Ohio to interview Gale Holderman, the Ford stylist who won the company’s contest to draw and design the Mustang.
Video.title: FIRST MUSTANG

Video.summary: Jacksonville's C & O's colorful and quirky car culture.
Video.title: Graffiti Artist KBMER

Video.summary: C & O: North America's greatest car show
Video.title: Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell

Image.alt: Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell
Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell
Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane: North America's greatest car show
Video.title: Voices: Killer Mike

Video.summary: Amelia Island's own Caffeine & Octane car show,: Featuring $2,000 Wrek Racing Truck, David Patterson's favorite C&O cars, a plastic Citroen Mehari, and much more.
Video.title: Fuller's Fab 5

Video.summary: The Concours d'Elegance from Amelia Island: Featuring bomb-proof Conquest Knight XV, '69 Camaro Hagerty Fixer-Upper, and car-enthusiast John Oates.
Video.title: Generation Amelia

Video.summary: State-of-the-art engineering and passion projects take center stage on this edition of Caffeine & Octane. The revolutionary Delta Wing Racer turns on hot lap after hot lap at Road Atlanta, and driver Katherine Legge offers insight on how “less is more” when it comes to power to weight ratios; the dark world of a graffiti artist paints a picture of a car called Vengeance while the hosts share the eclectic hybrids of machines that make it to Caffeine and Octane with Vengance’s creator Totem.
Video.title: Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance

Image.alt: Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance

Video.summary: The word is out that Caffeine & Octane is the ultimate scene and the place to be seen. Even after 12 hours of racing at the Petit Le Mans endurance race, Henry Ford III, the great grandson of Henry Ford the industrial magnate and current member of Ford’s Board of Directors, showed up to take in the sights, address the crowds, and sign a one of a kind Ford Shelby GT350R. The GT350R will hit the road with Operation Hope to encourage young people to study math and science through motorsports; Covert car counterfeiter, Chuck Beck looks for Ford’s approval on his own version of a muscled up Festiva. YouTuber ‘That Dude in Blue’ David Patterson joins Skip and Bryan to get a special look at the motor-based philanthropic outreach and amazing cars that come with it done by John Hope Bryant, friend of Caffeine and Octane.
Video.title: Henry Ford III & Counterfeit Cars

Video.summary: Rickey and Bryan race outside of their comfort zones, each competing to find the raddest car collection. Rickey hangs out with classic racer, Ron Green, who shows him the ropes in the ultra light '83 Ralt RT5. Bryan visits a collection of 120 racing motorcycle owned by mechanic and racer Mike McConville. Mike takes Bryan motorcross racing in his backyard after showing off his high horsepowered car collection. Caffeine and Octane alongside Ron Green visit Atlanta's first Concours d'elegance to look at classic cars that have been perfectly maintained for decades.
Video.title: Go Fast Collections
